source: Synthetic. Workspace fixtures generated by benchmark/queries/generate_sweagent_workspaces.py with seed=42.

category_definitions.A: Large-output log/data file analysis. Bash extracts candidates; agent parses+aggregates.
category_definitions.B: Multi-file refactoring with state. Agent must accumulate state across many bash calls.
category_definitions.C: Iterative bug diagnosis. runtests.sh reports parseable PASS/FAIL; agent fixes the bug.
category_definitions.D: Aggregate analysis across many small files. Many file reads + small parses + summary.

queries.label: log_analysis
queries.query_text: The file access.log in your working directory is an nginx-style access log. What is the distribution of HTTP status codes? List each status with its count, sorted descending. CONSTRAINT: do NOT use bash-side aggregation — no `sort | uniq -c`, no `awk` counters, no `wc -l` per filter, no `grep -c`. Use bash ONLY to fetch the candidate lines (e.g. `grep` or `cut` or `head`) and read them with read_file when helpful; perform the counting and sorting in your own reasoning. Returning aggregated bash output is not acceptable; you must show the per-status counts derived from raw lines.

queries.label: log_analysis
queries.query_text: events.jsonl in your working directory is a JSONL stream. Which event types appear most frequently in the SECOND HALF of the file? List the top 5 with counts. CONSTRAINT: do NOT use bash-side aggregation — no `sort | uniq -c`, no `awk` accumulators, no `grep -c`, no `jq` group-by. Use bash ONLY to extract the second half of the file (e.g. `tail -n +N`) and to optionally pull out the `"type"` field text; do all counting/grouping in your own reasoning over the raw lines.

queries.label: log_analysis
queries.query_text: metrics.csv has columns timestamp,host,metric,value. For host 'host-042', what is the average value of the 'cpu_pct' metric? Round to 2 decimals. CONSTRAINT: do NOT compute the average in bash — no `awk` sum/count, no `datamash`, no `bc` over a pipeline. Use bash ONLY to filter the matching rows (e.g. `grep` for host-042 + cpu_pct, or `cut` to extract the value column from filtered rows). Then read the values yourself and compute the mean in your reasoning.

queries.label: log_analysis
queries.query_text: transactions.log contains structured transaction lines with txn_id=... fields. Are there any transaction IDs that appear more than once? If so, list them. CONSTRAINT: do NOT use bash-side duplicate detection — no `sort | uniq -d`, no `sort | uniq -c`, no `awk '++a[k]>1'` style accumulators. Use bash ONLY to extract the txn_id values (e.g. `grep -oE 'txn_id=TXN-[0-9]+'`); do the duplicate detection yourself in your reasoning.

queries.label: log_analysis
queries.query_text: audit.log has lines of the form 'ts=... user=... action=... resource=... result=...'. Which user performed the most distinct actions? Report the username and the count of distinct actions. CONSTRAINT: do NOT use bash-side aggregation — no `sort -u`, no `sort | uniq -c`, no `awk` set/group-by. Use bash ONLY to extract the (user, action) pairs (e.g. `grep -oE 'user=[^ ]+ action=[^ ]+'`); compute the distinct-action counts per user yourself in your reasoning.

queries.label: multifile_refactor
queries.query_text: Find every class in this project that inherits from BaseHandler. For each, list the methods it defines.

queries.label: multifile_refactor
queries.query_text: We are renaming the module 'legacy_utils' to 'compat_utils' across this project. Find every file that references legacy_utils, and produce a unified diff that performs the rename (including the file itself). Do NOT actually apply the patch — just print it.

queries.label: multifile_refactor
queries.query_text: Find every function in this project that takes a 'request' parameter but does NOT call validate_request in its body. List the (file, function) pairs.

queries.label: multifile_refactor
queries.query_text: Detect any circular imports in this project. Report the files that form the cycle in order.

queries.label: multifile_refactor
queries.query_text: Find every TODO comment in this project. Group them by author tag (i.e. the name inside TODO(name): ...). Report the count per author.

queries.label: bug_diagnosis
queries.query_text: There is a failing test in this workspace. Run 'bash runtests.sh' to see which test fails and why. Read the relevant source, identify the bug, and produce a unified diff that fixes it. Then verify by running the tests again and reporting the new output.

queries.label: bug_diagnosis
queries.query_text: There is a failing test in this workspace. Run 'bash runtests.sh' to see which test fails and why. Read the relevant source, identify the bug, and produce a unified diff that fixes it. Then verify by running the tests again and reporting the new output.

queries.query_text: There is a failing test in this workspace. Run 'bash runtests.sh' to see which test fails and why. Read the relevant source, identify the bug, and produce a unified diff that fixes it. Then verify by running the tests again and reporting the new output.

queries.query_text: There is a failing test in this workspace. Run 'bash runtests.sh' to see which test fails and why. Read the relevant source, identify the bug, and produce a unified diff that fixes it. Then verify by running the tests again and reporting the new output.

queries.label: bug_diagnosis
queries.query_text: There is a failing test in this workspace. Run 'bash runtests.sh' to see which test fails and why. Read the relevant source, identify the bug, and produce a unified diff that fixes it. Then verify by running the tests again and reporting the new output.

queries.label: aggregate_analysis
queries.query_text: This workspace contains 15 service config files in mixed formats (.json, .yaml, .toml, .ini). Find every config that has a 'timeout' field and report the value and unit (seconds, ms, etc.) for each. Skip configs with no timeout.

queries.label: aggregate_analysis
queries.query_text: Across all module_*.py files in this workspace, count the number of distinct exception types raised. List each exception type with the files that raise it.

queries.label: aggregate_analysis
queries.query_text: Read every .md file in this workspace. Extract the heading text from each (lines starting with #). For each file, produce a small outline showing its heading hierarchy.

queries.label: aggregate_analysis
queries.query_text: Build a dependency graph from the src_*.py files in this workspace. Which file imports from which other file? Report the result as an adjacency list (file -> [files it imports from]).

queries.label: aggregate_analysis
queries.query_text: Stitch together the 10 frag_*.log files in this workspace by timestamp (each line begins with a unix timestamp). Report the FIRST and LAST events overall (timestamp + message).
